summary: Cycle L (12th overnight-triage fire of UTC 2026-05-23, ~4h 54min after cycle K — MAJOR CADENCE CHANGE from prior ~1h pattern) ran heartbeat-only. Both queues absent on disk for 33rd consecutive cycle. regen-all clean: ALL CHECKS PASSED, round-trip 0 failures sustained (27s elapsed at 19:01:58Z; heartbeat written 19:01:58Z status=PASS, 4-field schema with no `source` field). Three substantive findings beyond pure heartbeat: (1) CADENCE PATTERN BROKEN. K→L gap is ~4h 54min wall-clock (14:07:41Z cycle K commit → 19:01:15Z cycle L open). This is a major break from the ~1h cadence that held cycles G→K (5 consecutive ~1h cycles after E→F ~5h outlier). Carry-over #1 (cron-cadence flag, now 17th observation) ESCALATES from 'overwhelming natural variability' to 'pattern itself is uncertain — cron schedule may not be hourly'. Four possible interpretations: variable-interval, event-triggered, hourly-with-skipped-fires, manually-invoked. (2) HEARTBEAT STATUS=PASS at cycle L close (post-regen-all) refines GATE-FAIL diagnosis structurally. Cycle L's heartbeat has 4 fields (status, duration_seconds, last_pass_at_human, last_pass_at_utc) and NO `source` field. Compare to cycle K's pre-regen heartbeat (10 fields including `source='post-commit-hook'`, `head_sha`, `age_minutes`). This confirms TWO DISTINCT WRITE PATHS: (a) scripts/regen-all.ps1 writes PASS with 4-field minimal schema, no `source`; (b) .husky/post-commit hook writes with 10-field rich schema, `source='post-commit-hook'`, with GATE-FAIL on non-routine commit subjects (Overnight triage, feat, fix). The 6-observation GATE-FAIL pattern from cycles F→K is now STRUCTURALLY EXPLAINED. REFINED carry-over #7 captures this. (3) NO FOUNDER ACTIVITY IN K→L WINDOW. Last commit before cycle L was 954c3ed1 cron(routine) at 14:09:01Z (post-cycle-K auto-clean). No commits between then and cycle L open at 19:01Z (~4h 54min quiet). Founder was active in J→K window (ff4a47ae feat + 463148f8 fix(ci) just before cycle K) but has been quiet since. Cycle L does not race Founder activity. No commit-race during cycle L's regen-all run. Three pre-existing untracked Founder files unchanged from cycle K close (overnight-agent/reports/2026-05-23.md + tests/unit/animate.test.js + tests/unit/utils.test.js); left alone per isolation discipline. Single yellow user-context-gate continues (main-flows.html ~8.71d stale at cycle L, was 8.50d at K — natural drift over ~5h). WELLNESS REST FULLY DISCHARGED. Cycle K triggered rest at 14:05:00Z with rest_ends_after=14:35:00Z (30min minimum). Cycle L open at 19:01Z is ~4h 26min past rest minimum. Counters reset cleanly per counters_reset_on_resume schema. Status=active. Token consumption ~35k for cycle L well below 100k threshold. Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST — empty-inbox cycle honestly recorded; cadence break observed and flagged with intensified carry-over #1; GATE-FAIL dual-write-path model is direct evidence from heartbeat schema comparison; Founder K→L quiet correctly characterized. Eight carry-over Founder action items preserved from cycle K close (no new items this cycle; cadence observation is intensification of #1, GATE-FAIL refinement is intensification of #7).
founder_action_items: Cron cadence — now 17th consecutive flag, ESCALATED at cycle L close. K→L gap (~4h 54min) BREAKS the ~1h pattern that held cycles G→K (5 consecutive). The natural-variability interpretation no longer fits. Pattern itself is uncertain — cron schedule may not be hourly (interpretations: variable-interval, event-triggered, hourly-with-skipped-fires, manually-invoked). Founder-action remains: edit overnight-triage cron prompt to add Step-0 guard that exits clean if `git log --since='3h'` shows no inbox motion AND last regen-all heartbeat is < 60 min old. Founder's recent commits (ff4a47ae feat staging-push + 463148f8 fix CI) did NOT modify the overnight-triage cron prompt or schedule, so the guard remains unimplemented and the cadence diagnosis remains unverified.
A8_performance: Lighthouse 65/100 on 1 page (target 75+) - open .lighthouseci/*.html, fix top failures (carry-over from cycles S→K; dimension score holds due to rolling-window mechanics).

main-flows.html user-context capture ~8.71 days stale at cycle L (was 8.50 at cycle K — natural drift over ~5h) - run `node scripts/visual-audit/founder-context-capture.mjs` before next ship-close (not blocking; same diagnosis as cycles S→K).
quota-status weekly_cap field still null - no % computation possible (preserved Founder-triage item from cycles R→K; sidecar discipline OK per [quota-status-schema] check).

aggregate-fiq-status D40 parity GATE-FAIL - stale-timestamp >24h hard threshold tripped because FIQ has had zero churn across 33 consecutive empty-inbox cycles (~33h+ elapsed). NOT an aggregator bug. Will trip on every cycle until FIQ activity resumes or remedy lands (4 remedies enumerated in cycle B post-commit observation).

A12_operational - sustained yellow with continued oscillation across rolling-window mechanics. Cycle L ran clean; current dimension score visible in cycle L's regenerated docs/reports/app-health.html.

Heartbeat `status: GATE-FAIL` field semantics - STRUCTURALLY EXPLAINED at cycle L close via dual-write-path model. (a) scripts/regen-all.ps1 writes 4-field minimal schema (status, duration_seconds, last_pass_at_human, last_pass_at_utc), no `source` field, PASS when round-trip green. (b) .husky/post-commit hook writes 10-field rich schema (ts, timestamp, generated_at, last_pass_at_utc, last_pass_at_human, status, age_minutes, duration_seconds, head_sha, source), `source='post-commit-hook'`, with GATE-FAIL on non-routine commit subjects (Overnight triage, feat, fix). The 6-observation GATE-FAIL pattern from cycles F→K is now structurally explained. The routine-cron-doesn't-touch-heartbeat finding from cycles H/I/J/K is partially refined: routine-cron post-commit DOES write to heartbeat with PASS, but that PASS is overwritten by the next non-routine commit's GATE-FAIL write — OR routine-cron has a different post-commit branch that bypasses the check entirely. REFINED Founder-action: inspect `.husky/post-commit` source code to map which commit-subject patterns trigger the GATE-FAIL write path and whether routine-cron's write is overwritten or never happens. Cycle L stops short of inspecting hook source — Founder-decision boundary for governance change to remediation.

Wellness token-counter semantics inconsistency (preserved from cycle K close). Tokens accumulate across cron fires (continuous-persona semantics); hours_active does NOT accumulate (discrete-context semantics per cycle F discipline). Cycle L's clean reset confirms the inconsistency is benign in practice (rest cycle discharged cleanly, counters reset on resume), but the semantic mismatch persists. Founder-action: decide whether (a) both reset per cron fire (treating each as discrete) or (b) both accumulate (treating the persona as logically continuous). Cycle L honors current accumulating convention for tokens, discrete-context for hours_active.
